nodes.label: ADR: Export diagrammes drawio mxGraph XML
One-way drawio export
nodes.rationale: Users need a self-contained diagram export that opens in drawio desktop, drawio.com, VS Code, and similar tools without requiring a roundtrip import path.

nodes.label: Project diagram to mxGraph mapping
nodes.rationale: The exporter preserves project-specific node shapes, ports, labels, groups, and semantic metadata through mxGraph styles, geometry, points, and UserObject attributes.

nodes.label: ADR: Revert vendoring Tailwind and Font Awesome
CDN asset loading restored
nodes.rationale: The previous proxy constraint was lifted, so local vendoring no longer offset tarball size, update debt, export-template coupling, and noisy socket alerts.

nodes.label: ADR: Worklog convention in bilingual starter
AI handoff worklog
nodes.rationale: ADR history captures durable decisions but not operational task state, so current-task.md gives agents a stable recovery point.

nodes.label: ADR: Viewer validate button for frontmatter status
Frontmatter status validation
nodes.rationale: Humans can promote To be validated documents to Accepted directly from the viewer while keeping accuracy metadata refreshed when needed.

nodes.label: ADR: Read-only metadata for SuperSeeded documents
SuperSeeded metadata guard
nodes.rationale: Superseded ADRs remain historical records; refusing metadata mutations prevents stale documents from being revalidated accidentally.

nodes.label: ADR: Gate npm publish on tests CodeQL README sync
npm publish quality gate
nodes.rationale: Publishing waits for Playwright, CodeQL, and README sync so a tag cannot release an unverified package while workflows run in parallel.

nodes.label: ADR: Inline snippet editing from viewer
Inline snippet editing
nodes.rationale: Right-click editing and insertion keep Markdown as source of truth while letting users modify structured snippets through specialized mini-editors.

nodes.label: Vanilla frontend syntax check
nodes.rationale: The former vanilla frontend needed a fast dependency-free node --check pass because TypeScript build did not validate browser JavaScript syntax.

nodes.label: Classic frontend feature folders
nodes.rationale: Snippets and modals were grouped into explicit classic-script feature folders to reduce global frontend sprawl without introducing a bundler.

nodes.label: ADR: Sidebar status highlight tri-state pills
Sidebar lifecycle status pills
nodes.rationale: A tri-state sidebar toggle surfaces To be validated and SuperSeeded lifecycle states without forcing users to open each document.

nodes.label: HTML-in-canvas configuration prototype
nodes.rationale: The isolated prototype explored graph configuration interactions before being replaced by the production workspace.

nodes.label: ADR: Workspace configuration graph with LLM agents and MCP tool use
Workspace configuration graph
nodes.rationale: A persistent pan/zoom configuration graph lets users model LLM providers, agents, folders, prompts, MCP tool use, and runtime agent execution.

nodes.label: Diagram creation defaults
nodes.rationale: Persisting shape and arrow defaults in .living-doc.json makes project-wide visual conventions intentional while retaining localStorage as short-term memory.

nodes.label: ADR: Shared color picker swatch popup
Shared diagram color picker
nodes.rationale: A shared swatch popup replaces duplicated inline palettes and consistently respects runtime nodeColorOverrides across node, edge, and defaults UIs.

nodes.label: ADR: Edge style persistence and defaults
Edge style defaults
nodes.rationale: All edge types should share the same remembered direction, dashes, color, width, and font size so shape-to-shape edges match free-arrow behavior.

nodes.label: Immediate default synchronization
nodes.rationale: Saving defaults writes both project config and localStorage so the very next creation in the current session reflects the newly saved style.

nodes.label: Opinionated diagram STORAGE_DEFAULTS
nodes.rationale: New projects should start with differentiated, usable diagram styling instead of null defaults that fall back to hardcoded generic values.

nodes.label: Blueprint Prezi folder explorer
nodes.rationale: A read-only pan/zoom canvas gives spatial navigation over sourceRoot folders without exposing or editing source files.

nodes.label: Unified Vite Svelte 5 app
nodes.rationale: A single routed Svelte app replaces heterogeneous vanilla pages and globals, enabling shared components, runes i18n, explicit routing, and Vite builds.

nodes.label: ADR: Annotation popup scroll and hover fixes
Annotation popup scroll behavior
nodes.rationale: Annotation pills must open hidden details before scrolling and keep popups reachable long enough for deletion while hiding stale popups during scroll.

nodes.label: ADR: Resizable table of contents panel
Sticky resizable TOC panel
nodes.rationale: The TOC uses measured header height and sticky positioning to avoid scroll travel while keeping document navigation opt-in and resizable.

nodes.label: Accent-insensitive search pipeline
nodes.rationale: Applying NFD normalization and combining-mark removal consistently across sidebar filtering, full-text API, and in-document highlighting prevents accent mismatches.

hyperedges.label: Document viewer navigation and lifecycle affordances
